knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior_sections + active packet; zero Python outside packet.

exercises.code:
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
msg = MIMEText("Hola", "plain", "utf-8")
print(msg["Content-Type"])

from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart
from email.mime.application import MIMEApplication
msg = MIMEMultipart("mixed")
msg["Subject"] = "Test"
att = MIMEApplication(b"x", Name="a.txt")
att.add_header("Content-Disposition", "attachment", filename="a.txt")
msg.attach(att)
print("a.txt" in msg.as_string())

from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart from email.mime.text import MIMEText mixed = MIMEMultipart("mixed") alt = MIMEMultipart("alternative") alt.attach(MIMEText("hola", "plain")) alt.attach(MIMEText("hola", "html")) mixed.attach(alt) print(mixed.as_string().count("Content-Type:"))

import html
print(html.escape("<script>x</script>"))
import html name = "Ana" print("Hola {name}".format(name=html.escape(name)))

granted = ["mail.draft"]
print(all(x not in granted for x in ("mail.full", "admin")))

from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
past = (now - timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat()
future = (now + timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat()
for exp in (past, future):
    e = datetime.fromisoformat(exp)
    print("refresh" if e < now else "valid")

from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
expires_at = now - timedelta(seconds=1)
is_usable = now < expires_at
print(is_usable)

import re
pat = re.compile(r"^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$")
print(bool(pat.match("ana@example.pe")))
print(bool(pat.match("bad")))

DIRECTORY = {"C001": "ana@example.pe"}
em = DIRECTORY.get("C001")
dom = em.split("@")[-1] if em else ""
print("verified" if dom == "example.pe" else "rejected")

a, b = "ana.rojas@example.pe", "ana.rojas@example.com"
n = 0
for x, y in zip(a, b):
    if x != y:
        break
    n += 1
score = n / max(len(a), len(b))
print("match_no_es_fraude", round(score, 2))

xs = ["a@x", "b@x", "a@x"]
print(list(dict.fromkeys(xs)))

T = {"draft": {"submit": "pending"}}
state = "draft"
state = T[state]["submit"]
print(state)

T = {"draft": {"submit": "pending"}, "pending": {"approve": "approved"}}
state, action = "draft", "approve"
nxt = T.get(state, {}).get(action)
print(nxt if nxt else "invalid")

log = []
log.append({"from": "pending", "to": "approved", "actor": "rev1"})
print(log)

import hashlib
print(hashlib.sha256(b"run|to|v1").hexdigest()[:8])

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Theory: no real send — drafts/.eml sandbox + human approval.
Theory: high email similarity is weak contact evidence, not fraud proof.
Theory: least privilege = minimal OAuth scopes (e.g. draft).
Theory: idempotency key reuses same draft_id on retry.
